mainText: Good morning. Here is your AI briefing. Today we are seeing significant shifts in how enterprises and individuals engage with artificial intelligence. First, OpenAI has announced that GPT-5.6 is now the preferred model for Microsoft 365 Copilot. This upgrade is designed to bring stronger, faster performance to core applications like Word, Excel, and PowerPoint, helping users tackle their most ambitious projects with higher quality output. In the telecommunications sector, OpenAI is working with Deutsche Telekom to transition the company into an AI-native telco. This partnership is set to transform how they handle customer service, internal employee workflows, and even complex network operations, signaling a major shift in how large-scale infrastructure will rely on voice and AI integration moving forward. Meanwhile, OpenAI has also introduced ChatGPT Work, a new agent-based tool. Unlike traditional chatbots, this version is capable of taking independent actions across a user’s various apps and files. It is designed to stay with a project over several hours, helping to move tasks from a simple brainstorming phase all the way to completion. On the development front, Hugging Face continues to streamline the infrastructure for AI builders. They have recently enabled a one-click integration between their platform and Amazon SageMaker Studio, simplifying the transition from model hosting to managed compute. This move follows their focus on ensuring that developers can run AI workloads across any cloud environment while maintaining seamless data storage. Finally, OpenAI is making a push into education. Through a new initiative with the Walton Family Foundation, the OpenAI Academy is launching hands-on skills jams specifically for K-12 educators. The goal is to provide teachers with the practical AI skills necessary to effectively navigate and implement these tools within the classroom environment. These updates highlight a clear trend toward moving AI from a simple research tool into an active, operational partner for both massive global corporations and individual creators. That is your AI briefing for today.
